verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem et parabolam ad illos, quoniam oportet semper orare et non deficere,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ καὶ παραβολὴν αὐτοῖς πρὸς τὸ δεῖν πάντοτε προσεύχεσθαι καὶ μὴ ἐκκακεῖν,
verses.king_james: And he spake a parable unto them *to this end*, that men ought always to pray, and not to faint;

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Judex quidam erat in quadam civitate, qui Deum non timebat, et hominem non reverebatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων, κριτής τις ἦν ἔν τινι πόλει τὸν θεὸν μὴ φοβούμενος καὶ ἄνθρωπον μὴ ἐντρεπόμενος
verses.king_james: Saying, There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidua autem quædam erat in civitate illa, et veniebat ad eum, dicens: Vindica me de adversario meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χήρα δὲ ἦν ἐν τῇ πόλει ἐκείνῃ καὶ ἤρχετο πρὸς αὐτὸν λέγουσα ἐκδίκησόν με ἀπὸ τοῦ ἀντιδίκου μου
verses.king_james: And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nolebat per multum tempus. Post hæc autem dixit intra se: Etsi Deum non timeo, nec hominem revereor:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἤθελησεν ἐπὶ χρόνον μετὰ δὲ ταῦτα εἶπεν ἐν ἑαυτῷ εἰ καὶ τὸν θεὸν οὐ φοβοῦμαι καὶ ἄνθρωπον οὐκ ἐντρέπομαι
verses.king_james: And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;

verses.latin_vulgate: tamen quia molesta est mihi hæc vidua, vindicabo illam, ne in novissimo veniens sugillet me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διά γε τὸ παρέχειν μοι κόπον τὴν χήραν ταύτην ἐκδικήσω αὐτήν ἵνα μὴ εἰς τέλος ἐρχομένη ὑπωπιάζῃ με
verses.king_james: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem Dominus: Audite quid judex iniquitatis dicit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ κύριος ἀκούσατε τί ὁ κριτὴς τῆς ἀδικίας λέγει
verses.king_james: And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge saith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem non faciet vindictam electorum suorum clamantium ad se die ac nocte, et patientiam habebit in illis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς οὐ μὴ ποιήσει τὴν ἐκδίκησιν τῶν ἐκλεκτῶν αὐτοῦ τῶν βοώντων πρὸς αὐτόν ἡμέρας καὶ νυκτός καὶ μακροθυμῶν ἐπ᾽ αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico vobis quia cito faciet vindictam illorum. Verumtamen Filius hominis veniens, putas, inveniet fidem in terra?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ποιήσει τὴν ἐκδίκησιν αὐτῶν ἐν τάχει πλὴν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐλθὼν ἆρα εὑρήσει τὴν πίστιν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: I tell you that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem et ad quosdam qui in se confidebant tamquam justi, et aspernabantur ceteros, parabolam istam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ καὶ πρός τινας τοὺς πεποιθότας ἐφ᾽ ἑαυτοῖς ὅτι εἰσὶν δίκαιοι καὶ ἐξουθενοῦντας τοὺς λοιποὺς τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην
verses.king_james: And he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo homines ascenderunt in templum ut orarent: unus pharisæus et alter publicanus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄνθρωποι δύο ἀνέβησαν εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν προσεύξασθαι ὁ εἷς φαρισαῖος καὶ ὁ ἕτερος τελώνης
verses.king_james: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæus stans, hæc apud se orabat: Deus, gratias ago tibi, quia non sum sicut ceteri hominum: raptores, injusti, adulteri, velut etiam hic publicanus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ φαρισαῖος σταθεὶς πρὸς ἑαυτὸν ταῦτα προσηύχετο ὁ θεός εὐχαριστῶ σοι ὅτι οὐκ εἰμὶ ὥσπερ οἱ λοιποὶ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἅρπαγες ἄδικοι μοιχοί ἢ καὶ ὡς οὗτος ὁ τελώνης
verses.king_james: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men *are*, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.

verses.latin_vulgate: jejuno bis in sabbato, decimas do omnium quæ possideo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νηστεύω δὶς τοῦ σαββάτου ἀποδεκατῶ πάντα ὅσα κτῶμαι
verses.king_james: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et publicanus a longe stans, nolebat nec oculos ad cælum levare: sed percutiebat pectus suum, dicens: Deus propitius esto mihi peccatori.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ τελώνης μακρόθεν ἑστὼς οὐκ ἤθελεν οὐδὲ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν ἐπᾶραι ἀλλ᾽ ἔτυπτεν εἰς τὸ στῆθος αὐτοῦ λέγων, ὁ θεός ἱλάσθητί μοι τῷ ἁμαρτωλῷ
verses.king_james: And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as *his* eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico vobis, descendit hic justificatus in domum suam ab illo: quia omnis qui se exaltat, humiliabitur, et qui se humiliat, exaltabitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω ὑμῖν κατέβη οὗτος δεδικαιωμένος εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ ἢ ἐκεῖνος ὅτι πᾶς ὁ ὑψῶν ἑαυτὸν ταπεινωθήσεται ὁ δὲ ταπεινῶν ἑαυτὸν ὑψωθήσεται
verses.king_james: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified *rather* than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Afferebant autem ad illum et infantes, ut eos tangeret. Quod cum viderent discipuli, increpabant illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέφερον δὲ αὐτῷ καὶ τὰ βρέφη ἵνα αὐτῶν ἅπτηται ἰδόντες δὲ οἱ μαθηταὶ ἐπετίμησαν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And they brought unto him also infants, that he would touch them: but when *his* disciples saw *it*, they rebuked them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem convocans illos, dixit: Sinite pueros venire ad me, et nolite vetare eos: talium est enim regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς προσκαλεσάμενος αὐτὰ εἶπεν, ἄφετε τὰ παιδία ἔρχεσθαι πρός με καὶ μὴ κωλύετε αὐτά τῶν γὰρ τοιούτων ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But Jesus called them *unto him*, and said, Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen dico vobis, quicumque non acceperit regnum Dei sicut puer, non intrabit in illud.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὃς ἐὰν μὴ δέξηται τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ὡς παιδίον οὐ μὴ εἰσέλθῃ εἰς αὐτήν
verses.king_james: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogavit eum quidam princeps, dicens: Magister bone, quid faciens vitam æternam possidebo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπηρώτησέν τις αὐτὸν ἄρχων λέγων, διδάσκαλε ἀγαθέ τί ποιήσας ζωὴν αἰώνιον κληρονομήσω
verses.king_james: And a certain ruler asked him, saying, Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ei Jesus: Quid me dicis bonum? nemo bonus nisi solus Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς τί με λέγεις ἀγαθόν οὐδεὶς ἀγαθὸς εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? none *is* good, save one, *that is*, God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mandata nosti: non occides; non mœchaberis; non furtum facies; non falsum testimonium dices; honora patrem tuum et matrem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰς ἐντολὰς οἶδας μὴ μοιχεύσῃς μὴ φονεύσῃς μὴ κλέψῃς μὴ ψευδομαρτυρήσῃς τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα σου
verses.king_james: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait: Hæc omnia custodivi a juventute mea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ταῦτα πάντα ἐφύλαξαμην ἐκ νεότητος μου
verses.king_james: And he said, All these have I kept from my youth up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo audito, Jesus ait ei: Adhuc unum tibi deest: omnia quæcumque habes vende, et da pauperibus, et habebis thesaurum in cælo: et veni, sequere me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ταῦτα ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἕτι ἕν σοι λείπει πάντα ὅσα ἔχεις πώλησον καὶ διάδος πτωχοῖς καὶ ἕξεις θησαυρὸν ἐν οὐρανῷ, καὶ δεῦρο ἀκολούθει μοι
verses.king_james: Now when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, Yet lackest thou one thing: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.

verses.latin_vulgate: His ille auditis, contristatus est: quia dives erat valde.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀκούσας ταῦτα περίλυπος ἐγένετο ἦν γὰρ πλούσιος σφόδρα
verses.king_james: And when he heard this, he was very sorrowful: for he was very rich.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem Jesus illum tristem factum, dixit: Quam difficile, qui pecunias habent, in regnum Dei intrabunt!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ αὐτὸν ὁ ἰησοῦς περίλυπον γενόμενον εἶπεν πῶς δυσκόλως οἱ τὰ χρήματα ἔχοντες εἰσελεύσονται εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

verses.latin_vulgate: facilius est enim camelum per foramen acus transire quam divitem intrare in regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐκοπώτερον γάρ ἐστιν κάμηλον διὰ τρυμαλιᾶς ῥαφίδος εἰσελθεῖν ἢ πλούσιον εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελθεῖν
verses.king_james: For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt qui audiebant: Et quis potest salvus fieri?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον δὲ οἱ ἀκούσαντες καὶ τίς δύναται σωθῆναι
verses.king_james: And they that heard *it* said, Who then can be saved?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illis: Quæ impossibilia sunt apud homines, possibilia sunt apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν τὰ ἀδύνατα παρὰ ἀνθρώποις δυνατὰ ἐστιν παρὰ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem Petrus: Ecce nos dimisimus omnia et secuti sumus te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ πέτρος ἰδού, ἡμεῖς ἀφηκαμεν πάντα, καὶ ἠκολουθήσαμέν σοι
verses.king_james: Then Peter said, Lo, we have left all, and followed thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit eis: Amen dico vobis, nemo est qui reliquit domum, aut parentes, aut fratres, aut uxorem, aut filios propter regnum Dei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐδείς ἐστιν ὃς ἀφῆκεν οἰκίαν ἢ γονεῖς ἢ ἀδελφοὺς ἢ γυναῖκα ἢ τέκνα ἕνεκεν τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God's sake,

verses.latin_vulgate: et non recipiat multo plura in hoc tempore, et in sæculo venturo vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς οὐ μὴ ἀπολάβῃ πολλαπλασίονα ἐν τῷ καιρῷ τούτῳ καὶ ἐν τῷ αἰῶνι τῷ ἐρχομένῳ ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: Who shall not receive manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life everlasting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Assumpsit autem Jesus duodecim, et ait illis: Ecce ascendimus Jerosolymam, et consummabuntur omnia quæ scripta sunt per prophetas de Filio hominis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραλαβὼν δὲ τοὺς δώδεκα εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς ἰδού, ἀναβαίνομεν εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα καὶ τελεσθήσεται πάντα τὰ γεγραμμένα διὰ τῶν προφητῶν τῷ υἱῷ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: Then he took *unto him* the twelve, and said unto them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished.

verses.latin_vulgate: tradetur enim gentibus, et illudetur, et flagellabitur, et conspuetur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραδοθήσεται γὰρ τοῖς ἔθνεσιν καὶ ἐμπαιχθήσεται καὶ ὑβρισθήσεται καὶ ἐμπτυσθήσεται
verses.king_james: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on:

verses.latin_vulgate: et postquam flagellaverint, occident eum, et tertia die resurget.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μαστιγώσαντες ἀποκτενοῦσιν αὐτόν καὶ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ τρίτῃ ἀναστήσεται
verses.king_james: And they shall scourge *him*, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipsi nihil horum intellexerunt, et erat verbum istud absconditum ab eis, et non intelligebant quæ dicebantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτοὶ οὐδὲν τούτων συνῆκαν καὶ ἦν τὸ ῥῆμα τοῦτο κεκρυμμένον ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν καὶ οὐκ ἐγίνωσκον τὰ λεγόμενα
verses.king_james: And they understood none of these things: and this saying was hid from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, cum appropinquaret Jericho, cæcus quidam sedebat secus viam, mendicans.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ ἐγγίζειν αὐτὸν εἰς ἰεριχὼ τυφλός τις ἐκάθητο παρὰ τὴν ὁδὸν προσαιτῶν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum audiret turbam prætereuntem, interrogabat quid hoc esset.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ὄχλου διαπορευομένου ἐπυνθάνετο τί εἴη τοῦτο
verses.king_james: And hearing the multitude pass by, he asked what it meant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt autem ei quod Jesus Nazarenus transiret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπήγγειλαν δὲ αὐτῷ ὅτι ἰησοῦς ὁ ναζωραῖος παρέρχεται
verses.king_james: And they told him, that Jesus of Nazareth passeth by.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et clamavit, dicens: Jesu, fili David, miserere mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐβόησεν λέγων, ἰησοῦ υἱὲ δαβίδ, ἐλέησόν με
verses.king_james: And he cried, saying, Jesus, *thou* Son of David, have mercy on me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui præibant, increpabant eum ut taceret. Ipse vero multo magis clamabat: Fili David, miserere mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ προάγοντες ἐπετίμων αὐτῷ ἵνα σιωπήσῃ αὐτὸς δὲ πολλῷ μᾶλλον ἔκραζεν υἱὲ δαβίδ, ἐλέησόν με
verses.king_james: And they which went before rebuked him, that he should hold his peace: but he cried so much the more, *Thou* Son of David, have mercy on me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stans autem Jesus jussit illum adduci ad se. Et cum appropinquasset, interrogavit illum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σταθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐκέλευσεν αὐτὸν ἀχθῆναι πρὸς αὐτόν ἐγγίσαντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ἐπηρώτησεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And Jesus stood, and commanded him to be brought unto him: and when he was come near, he asked him,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Quid tibi vis faciam? At ille dixit: Domine, ut videam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων, τί σοι θέλεις ποιήσω ὁ δὲ εἶπεν κύριε ἵνα ἀναβλέψω
verses.king_james: Saying, What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Jesus dixit illi: Respice, fides tua te salvum fecit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἀνάβλεψον ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto him, Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et confestim vidit, et sequebatur illum magnificans Deum. Et omnis plebs ut vidit, dedit laudem Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παραχρῆμα ἀνέβλεψεν καὶ ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ δοξάζων τὸν θεόν καὶ πᾶς ὁ λαὸς ἰδὼν ἔδωκεν αἶνον τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw *it*, gave praise unto God.
